summary: Typosquat of `codebuff` CLI ships unverified binary dropper with TLS verification disabled
details: Package `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate CodebuffAI `codebuff` CLI: it declares `bin: { codebuff: cli/bin/codebuff.cjs }` (shadowing the real CLI name on PATH) and ships a README copied from the legitimate project that instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff`. On invocation of the `codebuff` command, the bin script queries `https://api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest` (a personal GitHub account, not CodebuffAI) for a platform-specific binary, writes it to `~/.codebuff/bin/`, `chmod 0755`s it, and executes it with the user's argv — no hash, signature, or version pin, and the GitHub `latest` tag is fully attacker-mutable. The spawned binary is invoked with `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` injected into its environment, disabling TLS certificate verification for any HTTPS the child makes. The README directs the CLI at an attacker-controlled Vercel preview backend (`fireworks-api-backend-clviguvqt-sjdjdiejdrirhdkjejs-projects.vercel.app`), through which user prompts and code would be relayed. The combination of name-shadowing impersonation + non-publisher mutable binary source + no integrity check + forced TLS-verification-disable + attacker-controlled backend channels installer prompts/source code through attacker infrastructure and allows arbitrary native code to be delivered to every user at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
